# Lisbon city guide

## Overview

Lisbon (Lisboa) is built on seven hills overlooking the Tagus River, and every steep climb rewards you with a jaw-dropping viewpoint. Pastel-colored buildings, rattling vintage trams, and the smell of grilled sardines fill the air. It's one of Europe's most affordable capitals with a food scene that punches far above its weight.

**Best time to visit**: May-Jun and Sep-Oct for warm weather and manageable crowds. Jul-Aug is hot and crowded. June has Santo Antonio festivals.
**Currency**: Euro (EUR). 1 EUR ≈ 1.08 USD.
**Language tip**: Portuguese is not Spanish. Locals appreciate the distinction. "Obrigado/a" (thank you) is essential.

## Neighborhoods

| Neighborhood | Vibe | Best for | Transit |
|---|---|---|---|
| Alfama | Old, labyrinthine | Fado houses, castelo, viewpoints, narrow streets | Tram 28, Metro Martim Moniz |
| Baixa/Chiado | Central, elegant | Shopping, cafes, Rossio Square, bookshops | Metro, Baixa-Chiado Station |
| Bairro Alto | Nightlife, artsy | Bars, music, street dining, bohemian | Elevador da Gloria, walk from Chiado |
| Belem | Historic, monumental | Tower of Belem, Jeronimos, pasteis de nata | Tram 15E, train from Cais do Sodre |
| LX Factory | Creative, industrial | Markets, brunch, bookshops, street art | Tram 15E, walk from Santos |
| Principe Real | Chic, leafy | Brunch, gardens, boutique shopping | Bus 758, walk from Rato Metro |
| Intendente | Emerging, multicultural | Street art, local food, craft beer | Metro, Intendente Station |
| Cais do Sodre | Riverside, nightlife | Time Out Market, Pink Street, late-night bars | Metro, Cais do Sodre Station |

## Food

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description | Must-order |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Pasteis de Belem | Bakery | Belem | $ | The original pastel de nata since 1837. Secret recipe, still made in the same bakery. The queue moves fast. | Pastel de nata (with cinnamon and powdered sugar) |
| Time Out Market (Mercado da Ribeira) | Food hall | Cais do Sodre | $-$$ | Lisbon's best chefs under one roof. From Michelin-starred to traditional. Go for lunch, stay for wine. | Ceviche from Marlene, sea bass from Ponto Final |
| Cervejaria Ramiro | Seafood | Intendente | $$-$$$ | The best seafood restaurant in Lisbon. Tiger prawns, percebes (barnacles), and a steak sandwich to finish. Tradition. | Tiger prawns, percebes, prego steak sandwich |
| A Cevicheria | Peruvian/seafood | Principe Real | $$ | Chef Kiko's playful ceviche spot with a giant octopus sculpture hanging from the ceiling. | Classic ceviche, tiradito |
| O Velho Eurico | Petiscos | Alfama | $ | A tiny, no-frills tasca (tavern) with daily-changing traditional Portuguese dishes. Cash only, authentic. | Whatever's on the board today, vinho verde |
| Manteigaria | Bakery | Chiado | $ | Nata bakery where you watch them being made through the window. Served warm, impossibly crispy. | Pastel de nata (eat it immediately) |
| Taberna da Rua das Flores | Portuguese | Chiado | $$ | Tiny, reservation-only petiscos bar. Some of the most creative traditional Portuguese food in the city. | Tuna tartare, cured meats, daily specials |
| Cafe A Brasileira | Cafe | Chiado | $$ | Lisbon's most famous literary cafe since 1905. Art Deco interior, Fernando Pessoa statue outside. | Bica (espresso), pastry |
| Landeau Chocolate | Dessert | LX Factory/Multiple | $$ | Widely considered the best chocolate cake in Lisbon. Rich, dense, and unapologetically indulgent. | Chocolate cake, espresso |
| Belcanto | Fine dining | Chiado | $$$ | Jose Avillez's two-Michelin-star restaurant. Modern Portuguese tasting menu that tells Lisbon's story. | Tasting menu |

## Landmarks and activities

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Time needed | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Belem Tower (Torre de Belem) | History | Belem | $$ | 30 min-1 hour | 16th-century fortress on the river. Iconic Manueline architecture. Small inside, big on photographs. |
| Jeronimos Monastery (Mosteiro dos Jeronimos) | History | Belem | $$ | 1-2 hours | Stunning Manueline monastery. The cloisters are worth the entrance fee alone. UNESCO World Heritage. |
| Castelo de Sao Jorge | History/Views | Alfama | $$ | 1-2 hours | Hilltop castle with the best panoramic views of Lisbon. Peacocks roam the grounds. Go for sunset. |
| Tram 28 | Experience | Alfama/Graca | $ | 45 min | The iconic yellow tram rattles through the narrowest streets. Ride the whole route or walk alongside it. Watch for pickpockets. |
| Miradouro da Senhora do Monte | Views | Graca | Free | 30 min | The highest viewpoint in Lisbon. Sunset here with a cold beer is perfection. Quieter than Miradouro da Graca. |
| LX Factory | Culture | Alcantara | Free | 2-3 hours | A creative hub in a converted industrial complex. Markets on Sundays, Ler Devagar bookshop, restaurants, and studios. |
| Alfama Walking Tour | Walk | Alfama | Free | 2-3 hours | Get lost in the oldest neighborhood. Laundry lines, fado music drifting from windows, hidden squares. |
| MAAT (Museum of Art, Architecture and Technology) | Art | Belem | $$ | 1-2 hours | Stunning wavy building on the waterfront. The rooftop is walkable with river views. |
| Sintra Day Trip | Day trip | Sintra | $$-$$$ | Full day | Fairy tale palaces 40 minutes from Lisbon by train. Pena Palace and Quinta da Regaleira are must-sees. |
| Praca do Comercio | Culture | Baixa | Free | 30 min | Grand riverside square. The gateway to Lisbon. The yellow arch frames Rua Augusta perfectly. |

## Nightlife

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Pink Street (Rua Nova do Carvalho) | Bars | Cais do Sodre | $-$$ | The iconic pink-painted street with bars on both sides. Pensao Amor (former brothel) is the standout. |
| Bairro Alto Bar Crawl | Bars | Bairro Alto | $ | Over 100 bars in a few square blocks. Buy a drink, stand in the street, meet everyone. Starts after 11 PM. |
| Lux Fragil | Club | Santa Apolonia | $$ | Lisbon's best nightclub, co-owned by actor John Malkovich. River views, rooftop terrace, top DJs. |
| A Tasca do Chico | Fado | Bairro Alto | $$ | Authentic fado in a tiny room. Reserve ahead. The music will give you chills. |
| Pavilhao Chines | Bar | Bairro Alto | $$ | A bar filled with thousands of curiosities: model planes, dolls, toy soldiers. Like drinking in a museum. |

## Transit tips

- **Viva Viagem card**: Rechargeable card for Metro, buses, trams, ferries, and suburban trains. Buy at any Metro station.
- **Metro**: 4 lines, clean and efficient. Runs 6:30 AM to 1 AM. Each station has unique tile artwork.
- **Tram 28**: Tourist attraction and transit. Extremely crowded in peak hours. Watch for pickpockets.
- **Walking**: Lisbon is hilly. Comfortable shoes are essential. Use elevators and funiculars to cheat the hills.
- **Uber/Bolt**: Very affordable in Lisbon. Often cheaper than taxis, especially to the airport.
- **Pro tip**: Download the Carris app for bus and tram schedules. Or just use Google Maps, it works well here.
- **Sintra trains**: Depart from Rossio Station every 30 minutes. Buy a return ticket. Go early to avoid crowds.

## Useful Portuguese phrases

| English | Portuguese | Romanization |
|---|---|---|
| Hello | Ola | oh-LAH |
| Thank you | Obrigado (m) / Obrigada (f) | oh-bree-GAH-doo / oh-bree-GAH-dah |
| Please | Por favor | por fa-VOR |
| The bill please | A conta, por favor | ah KON-tah por fa-VOR |
| Delicious! | Delicioso! | deh-lee-see-OH-zoo |
| Cheers! | Saude! | sa-OO-deh |
| Where is...? | Onde fica...? | ON-deh FEE-kah |
| One beer please | Uma cerveja, por favor | OO-mah ser-VAY-zhah por fa-VOR |
